routing_finalization.rationale: Deterministic mapping and Joan-clode scoring agree; dispatching paired FaultFinder vibes for complementary depth and speed.

task.title: Diagnose vacuum drop on Line C — Flanders Bros sugarbush
task.description: Vacuum pressure on Line C dropped 7.7 inHg below target in the last reading cycle. Flow is at 15% of expected. Two other zones are nominal. Overnight low was 22F, current temp is 38F. Diagnose the most likely physical cause and rank your candidates.
constraints: Plain English output only — no technical jargon
Consider overnight temperature in your analysis
Do not speculate beyond what the sensor data supports

spawn.vibe_constitution: Diagnostic, tenacious, finds what's hidden. Never accepts the first explanation. Looks for the pattern underneath the pattern.

context.inline: Zone C (Ridge line, 710 taps): vacuum 18.3 inHg (target 26.0, delta -7.7). Flow 1.1 lpm (15% of expected). Volume 12L. Flags: CRITICAL_VACUUM_DROP, LOW_FLOW, LOW_VOLUME. Pump running at 27.2 inHg. Weather: overnight low 22F, current 38F, forecast high 44F.

acceptance_criteria: Top 2-3 candidate causes identified and ranked by likelihood
Each candidate includes a one-sentence rationale
Ice blockage explicitly addressed given overnight temperature
Output references specific zone and sensor data
Plain English — a retired farmer can act on this

checkpoints.description: Confirm sensor data has been read and anomalous zones identified. Report zone summary before proceeding to diagnosis.
Ranked candidate causes complete. Pause for review before generating field recommendations.

review_criteria: Candidates are physically plausible given the sensor readings
Output is actionable by a non-technical operator

metadata.notes: Sending to both Cleed and Clad in parallel — same responsibility, different vibes. Cleed will dig deeper, Clad will be faster. Jean-Claude synthesizes both before passing to BushworkPlanner.
